chief_complaint: A woman in her 50s presented with intermittent chest tightness, dyspnoea, and vomiting for 4 days.
history: The patient has a background of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease secondary to smoking.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are provided.
labs: Dynamic ischaemic ECG changes, elevation in high-sensitivity cardiac troponin T levels, and left ventriculography suggestive of mid-wall variant Takotsubo cardiomyopathy. Thyroid function tests consistent with hyperthyroidism, and elevated thyroid-stimulating hormone receptor antibody.
correct_diagnosis: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy in the setting of thyroid storm
differentials: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy in the setting of thyroid storm
correct_ranking: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy in the setting of thyroid storm
